# Mutator coverage — CommandHandler vs. domain modules

**Date:** 2026-07-19. Generated by scanning every mutating (`POST`/`PUT`/`PATCH`/`DELETE`)
route across `routes/kingdom-*.js` and `routes/hero.js` for `commandHandler.handle({ type: … })`
calls, cross-referenced against `game/command-handler.js`'s actual `COMMAND_TYPES` and
`handle()` switch. This is a snapshot — re-run the classification below if routes change;
do not hand-edit the tables without re-verifying.

## Policy (A5-2)

**`CommandHandler` is the mutator boundary for the classic, `engine.js`-rooted simulation
systems** — turn, combat, spell, covert-\*, expeditions, hire/recruit, build-queue actions,
study/select-school, purchase-upgrade, calculate-score, raid-trade-route, forge-tools
(legacy smithy hammers/scaffolding, not the Forge & Lava Industry system), award-xp/troop-xp.
`check:command-boundary` enforces this by forbidding kingdom-\*/auth/hero/admin route files
from `require('../game/engine')` or calling its listed forbidden mutators directly.

**Newer, already-modularized systems are not required to route through `CommandHandler`** —
Forge & Lava Industry (`game/forge-upgrades.js`, `game/forge-production.js`,
`game/flux-barge.js`, `game/lava-vents.js`, `game/lava-expedition.js`), Dragon Evolution
(`game/evolution/`), Prestige rebirth (`game/prestige/`), and attunements/synergies
(fragment-bonus-manager + related managers) mutate via their own dedicated domain module
called directly from the route, wrapped in the route's own transaction. This is intentional,
not a gap, **as long as**:

1. The route never reaches into `game/engine.js`'s forbidden mutators (already enforced by
   `check:command-boundary` for these files).
2. The domain module is a real, single-purpose module (not scattered inline route logic) —
   true for every system in this category today.
3. Multi-write mutations wrap in `db.withTransaction`.

**Why not force everything through `CommandHandler` instead (the alternative, rejected):**
Prestige rebirth is the concrete precedent for why not — `game/command-handler.js`'s
`handlePrestige()` deliberately throws (`"Prestige rebirth must use POST /api/kingdom/rebirth
(atomic wipe). CommandHandler prestige is disabled."`) rather than dispatching, because
rebirth's `FOR UPDATE` row lock + atomic multi-table wipe transaction doesn't fit
`CommandHandler`'s simple `handle(type, payload) → { updates, events }` shape without
`CommandHandler` itself growing transaction-management responsibility it doesn't otherwise
have. Retrofitting Forge/Evolution/Prestige/attunements into `COMMAND_TYPES` would be a
large, purely mechanical migration of already-correct, already-tested code for no behavior
change and real regression risk — rejected per "no path of least resistance" *in the other
direction*: forcing a one-size-fits-all mechanism onto systems that already have a clean,
correct, differently-shaped boundary is not actually simpler, just uniform for its own sake.

**Socket exception (tracked separately, not this policy):** `game/sockets.js` calls
`engine.resolveMilitaryAttack`/`engine.castSpell` directly — that *is* a real gap (it's the
exact class of call HTTP routes are forbidden from making, and `check:command-boundary`
doesn't scan `game/sockets.js` at all). See `TODO.md` A5-4/A5-5. Not covered by the policy
above because sockets bypass `game/engine.js`'s forbidden-mutator list entirely, rather than
correctly using a dedicated domain module the way Forge/Evolution/Prestige do.

**Dead command types removed as part of this pass:** `process-build-queue` — confirmed zero
real callers, redundant (build-queue advancement already happens automatically every turn
inside `processTurn`, `game/construction.js`'s `processBuildQueue(k, events, xpSourcesAccum)`
call), and **actively broken** if it had ever been called: `handleProcessBuildQueue(kingdom,
db)` called `engine.processBuildQueue(kingdom, db)`, passing the DB adapter where the real
function signature expects an `events` array — would have thrown a `TypeError` on first use.
Removed from `COMMAND_TYPES`, its `case`, and its handler method.

**Kept as intentional, not dead:** `prestige` stays in `COMMAND_TYPES` — it is a working
fence, not unused code the way `process-build-queue` was. See the policy section above.

## Coverage matrix

`CH:<type>` = routed through `commandHandler.handle({ type: '<type>' }, …)`.
`direct+txn` = calls its own domain module(s) directly, wrapped in `db.withTransaction`.
`direct` = calls its own domain module(s)/`db` directly, no explicit multi-statement
transaction wrapper found — for most of these that's correct (a single `UPDATE` via
`applyUpdates`/`db.run` is already atomic at the DB level on its own); this column is
descriptive, not a verdict that anything here is unsafe. A real transaction-safety audit
of the `direct` rows is separate, follow-on work, not part of this pass.

### `routes/kingdom-build.js` (17 mutating routes)

| Route | Path |
|---|---|
| CH:queue-buildings | `POST /build-queue` |
| direct+txn | `POST /training-allocation` |
| direct+txn | `POST /build-allocation` |
| direct+txn | `POST /resource-build-allocation` |
| direct | `POST /school-allocation` |
| CH:demolish-building | `POST /demolish` |
| direct+txn | `POST /build` |
| direct+txn | `POST /cancel-building` |
| direct+txn | `POST /smithy/buy-hammers` |
| direct+txn | `POST /smithy/buy-scaffolding` |
| (no DB write — validation only) | `POST /smithy-allocation` |
| direct+txn | `POST /tower-craft` |
| direct | `POST /tower-cancel` |
| direct | `POST /tower-allocation` |
| direct | `POST /shrine-allocation` |
| direct | `POST /mausoleum-allocation` |
| direct | `POST /buy-mausoleum-upgrade` |

### `routes/hero.js` (1 mutating route)

| Route | Path |
|---|---|
| CH:recruit-hero | `POST /recruit` |

## Summary

- 83 mutating routes across `kingdom-*` + `hero`. 13 route through `CommandHandler`.
  70 mutate via their own domain module + route-level transaction — this is the expected,
  policy-sanctioned shape for everything except the classic engine-rooted systems above.
- Zero routes reach into `game/engine.js`'s forbidden mutators directly (`check:command-boundary`
  already enforces and confirms this).
- The one real gap is `game/sockets.js`, which is outside this table entirely (it's not a
  kingdom route) — see `TODO.md` A5-4/A5-5.
